title: Stats NZ survey charter: Our commitment to you | Stats NZ
content:
## Stats NZ survey charter: Our commitment to you
This charter sets out how we will work with you if you’re required to participate in one of our surveys. It outlines why we need to carry out surveys, what you can expect, and how to get help if you need it.

## Why Stats NZ surveys are important
We carry out surveys to collect the data needed to produce vital information for New Zealand.
As well as the census, we survey people and businesses on topics such as income, housing, the environment, economic growth, and social well-being.
Our statistics give local councils, community groups, businesses, iwi, and government the information they need to make decisions about the services they provide.
That’s why, as for the census, everyone in New Zealand selected for one of our surveys must take part. This is a legal requirement under the Data and Statistics Act 2022.
Our statistics are independent and impartial. We can’t produce them without your help.
When you’re participating in one of our surveys you can expect us to:
- keep your information secure and confidential
- be prompt, professional, courteous, and easy to deal with
- show you photo identification if we visit for a face-to-face interview
- let you know how much time an interview will take and set up a time that suits you
- be responsive to your individual and cultural needs
- explain how we will use the information you provide.
We ask you to please:
- answer questions as accurately as possible
- return the electronic or paper questionnaires by the date requested
- provide a suitable place for our interviewer to use their laptop if they visit you
- make the time to answer the survey if we phone you.
We want to hear about your experience. We value feedback and will use it to improve how we work with survey participants.
We will listen to any concerns you raise, and respond quickly and fairly.
We can also answer questions about completing a survey.
Contact us about a survey
Page updated 11 March 2024
